name: Rethink Ireland
description: Rethink Ireland is a venture philanthropy organization supporting innovative non-profit organisations in Ireland

name: Helium Arts
description: Uses arts-based therapy for children with illnesses.

name: Hope and Courage Collective
description: Fights hate speech and extremism in Ireland.

name: HSE (Health Service Executive)
description: Partners in funding mental health initiatives and healthcare programs.

name: Tacú Ballinrobe Family Resource Centre
description: Awardee supported by Rethink Ireland in various impact areas.

name: Liquid Therapy
description: Liquid Therapy is a multi-award winning charity which provides inclusive and adaptive surf therapy programmes which are beneficial to our physical and mental health. Our mission is that the mental health and wellbeing benefits of the Ocean and surfing are available to all.

name: Cruthu Foundation Way2Work
description:
Way 2 Work Ireland (W2W) is a not-for-profit organisation fully funded by Tusla the Child and Family Agency offering supports such as individual and tailored assistance through a mentor / mentee, training, and employment programme. W2W Ireland  deals specifically with young people (aged 16-23) who have been referred by Tusla. W2W was established on the 1st of January 2019 to work in partnership with Tusla Child and Family Agency and private enterprises nationwide.  

Mission
To support young people’s ambitions and create opportunities for all young people irrespective of privilege or background.

Vision
To create a sustainable solution for the provision of apprenticeships and employment by facilitating engagement between state agencies and the private sector. This is fully supported by our mentorship and training programmes for successful outcomes.

name: Spraoi & Sport
description:
Spraoi agus Spórt is an award-winning social enterprise based in Carndonagh, Inishowen, Co. Donegal. We provide services and activities for young families, children, young people, and adults that are inclusive of disability and/or autism.

We work to address disadvantage, strengthen our community and enhance opportunities in this region.

name: Special Olympics
description: Special Olympics Ireland, provides year-round sports training, athletic competition, and developmental programmes for almost 7000 athletes annually. Giving our athletes continuing opportunities to develop physical fitness, demonstrate courage, experience joy and participate in a sharing of gifts, skills and friendships with their families, other Special Olympics athletes and the community. Special Olympics Ireland is proud to be an all island programme made up of 5 Regions which govern different geographical areas and is supported by a  team in a central office. They are Connaught, Eastern, Leinster, Munster and Ulster Regions.

name: First Fortnight
description: First Fortnight Ltd is an Irish charity dedicated to challenging mental health stigma through arts and cultural action. Established in 2009, the organization aims to make the first two weeks of each year synonymous with mental health awareness by hosting the First Fortnight Festival, an annual event featuring a diverse range of artistic performances, workshops, and discussions. Awardee supported by Rethink Ireland in various impact areas.

name: Tacu Family Resource Centre
description: Tacú FRC promotes and operates a family resource centre and development programme, which  acts as a focus and catalyst for development for the community of Ballinrobe and surrounding areas

name: TUSLA
description:
Public Sector Partner & Funder:TUSLA is Ireland’s national agency responsible for improving wellbeing and outcomes for children. It oversees child protection, family support, and early intervention services. Child and Family Agency, collaborative safeguarding efforts.

In collaboration with First Fortnight, TUSLA supports safeguarding and referral processes for vulnerable children receiving creative arts therapy.

name: Focus Ireland
description:
Mental Health Service Providers & NGOs: Focus Ireland is one of Ireland’s leading charities working to prevent homelessness. It provides housing, support services, and advocacy for families and individuals experiencing or at risk of homelessness. 

Therapy programme development (summer groups). Their partnership with First Fortnight includes referral pathways and joint programme development, particularly for children and youth. One of our location for child & adolescent as well as adult service.

name: Community Foundation Ireland
description:
Community Foundation Ireland is a philanthropic organisation that connects donors with charitable causes across the country. It supports long-term social change by funding community-based initiatives, including projects that promote mental health, social inclusion, and the arts.

Philanthrophic hub, NGO, working on projects, one of which is Begin Together,  a year-long initiative to provide group creative arts therapy for children and young people who are experiencing or at risk of homelessness in Ireland. The project will focus on delivering 60 therapy sessions across three key locations in Dublin and Meath.

name: Blue Box Creative Arts Centre
description:
Mental Health Service Providers & NGOs: Blue Box is a Limerick-based therapeutic centre providing creative arts therapies to children and young people in schools and communities. It is known for its focus on trauma-informed, early intervention. 

Limerick-based therapeutic partner. As a therapy location and local partner, it enhances First Fortnight’s reach and impact in the region.

name: Donegal Domestic Violence Refuge (Gloria House)
description:
Mental Health Service Providers & NGOs: Gloria House is a domestic violence refuge in Donegal offering emergency accommodation and support for women and children fleeing abuse.

Direct therapy location and support setting. It partners with First Fortnight to deliver trauma-informed creative arts therapy within its safe space, supporting recovery, resilience, and emotional healing for residents.

name: Knocklee House (Kerry Recovery Centre)
description:
Knocklee House is an 8-bed supported facility in Tralee providing semi‑independent living with tailored care plans and intensive support for people experiencing homelessness with mental health challenges.

Knocklee House is a strategic partner in First Fortnight’s efforts to expand creative therapies to vulnerable adults through trusted local services.

name: St. Brigid’s Family Resource Centre (Tralee)
description:
St. Brigid’s FRC is a community hub in Tralee that delivers a wide range of supports including parenting classes, youth services, and wellbeing programmes. 

St Brigits is a Partner of First Fortnight for upcoming Kerry-based service. As a future therapy site for First Fortnight, it plays a key role in local outreach, referrals, and engagement with families in need.

The centre offers a range of family and community supports, including parenting programmes, a parent and toddler group, full-day childcare for children aged 1-12 years, adult education courses, support groups, assistance for the unemployed, and advice and information services. Its primary aims are to enhance the economic, social, cultural, and environmental development of the area by assessing local needs and delivering tailored programmes, as well as to combat disadvantage by improving family functioning.

name: Crosscare
description:
Crosscare supports people who are homeless or struggling to find accommodation and who may have complex needs. They run seven homeless services, to a very high standard, while all the time working to help people move on to a better, more stable life.

Crosscare Haven House  provides Supported Temporary Accommodation (STA) for six months to individuals who are homeless that present with complex, medium to high support needs. Crosscare is a location for First Fortnight adult creative art therapy. 

A broad range of social care services in Dublin, including pioneering foodbanks, homelessness accommodation/support, migrant integration and youth services as a social care charity rooted in a Catholic ethos

name: NOVAS
description:
NOVAS is a voluntary organisation that supports vulnerable individuals experiencing homelessness, addiction, or mental health issues. Active across Ireland, it offers housing, health services, and outreach.

NOVAS helps people experiencing homelessness move into stable accommodation and sustain tenancies by delivering intensive home‑based support and move‑on services.

NOVAS is a key partner in embedding First Fortnight’s therapies within trusted local care frameworks, especially in Limerick and Kerry.

name: GP Practices and Primary Care Centres
description:
Local GPS from the Primary Care Centre in Ballinrobe and Rural GPS

The Local GPS do not make a referal directly but do send peole into the Youth Link Worker as well as to Tacú for support.

name: Young People
description: Attend the SPACE Project as well as other service in the Maple YC

name: Parents
description: Accompaining their child to service for initial intake and other agreed meetings

name: Youthreach
description:
Cater for the Educational needs of Young People whom mainstream school does not meet their needs

Actively refer people into service and open to getteing extra group support

name: Education Welfare Officers (EWOs, TESS team)
description:
Support for young people struggling with school 

They make direct referels into the Youth Link worker as well as to Tacú other supports.

name: School Guidance Counsellors/Chaplins/School Support Team
description:
Support Young people in the 3 secondary schools in our catchment area 

They make direct referels into the Youth Link worker as well as to Tacú other supports.

name: JLOs/ Managers/Support staff in International Protection Centre?
description:
Support Young people and their families in local IPAS Centres

They make direct referels into the Youth Link worker as well as to Tacú other supports.

name: Children's Health Ireland
description:
CHI oversees and delivers specialised hospital care. We work with services in Galway and Dublin

Way 2 Work support CHI patients transitioning from long-term medical or mental health services into education or employment, fostering resilience and inclusion.

name: ETBs
description:
Provide education and training

ETBs are vital partners for upskilling and qualifications. Way 2 Work often refers participants to ETB courses

name: Sisk Group
description:
One of Ireland’s largest construction companies, active in infrastructure, commercial, and residential sectors; committed to corporate social responsibility.

A recruitment partner of Way 2 Work, Sisk provides work placements and job opportunities for participants, particularly in entry-level and apprentice roles.

name: Construction Industry Federation
description:
National representative body for the Irish construction sector, supporting skills development, safety, and industry standards

CIF helps inform employment pathways and skills needs in the construction sector; supports linkages between employers and social initiatives like Way 2 Work.

name: Society of the Irish Motor Industry (SIMI)
description:
The representative body for the motor industry in Ireland, including vehicle sales, repair, and parts distribution sectors.

Informs of apprenticeship and job opportunities

name: Parents, Guardians & Aftercare workers
description: Provide emotional and logistic support

name: DEIS Schools
description: Schools under the DEIS program receive targeted supports to tackle educational disadvantage and improve outcomes for students.

name: Oberstown Children Detention Campus
description: Oberstown Children Detention Campus is a youth detention centre located in the north part of County Dublin, Ireland. It houses both male and female offenders

name: Involve CLG
description:
nvolve Youth and Community Services was founded in 1972 as a voluntary committee, and established as a limited company with charitable status in 1988.  Involve went through a rebranding and reconfiguration of the organisation during 2011, with the head office and the organisations name being officially changed from NATC and launched by the then Minister for Children and Youth Affairs, Francis Fitzgerald on July 8th 2011. The Organisation’s original function was the representative body for Senior Traveller Training Centres with Government Departments and other Statutory and Voluntary Organisations, the last of these centres being phased out in 2012.

Involves history is embedded in education and training. Today, Involve is a youth, community and communications organisation working primarily with members of the Traveller community. It is the main provider of youth work services to young Travellers in Ireland.  As part of the delivery of this service we also focus on young people from marginalised backgrounds.

name: Arts Council
description: Funds the development of the arts in Ireland

name: Children and young persons committees (CYPSC)
description: Tusla run. Cross sector support for children's services in each county.

name: Regional Health Areas
description: The HSE management areas. There are 6.

name: Family Resource Centres
description: Community centres delivering wrap around service for their immediate communities of need.

name: Home Liaison Officers
description: Supports children who are not able to attend school

name: Hospital clinical staff
description: Doctors, nurses, physios etc.

name: Patient Support Groups
description: Other condition focussed charities that support children ie Diabetes Ireland

name: Sláintecare Healthy Communities (Donegal)
description: Government-led initiative providing targeted wellbeing and prevention programmes in disadvantaged communities.

name: Jigsaw
description: Youth mental health service offering free, confidential support for 12–25-year-olds

name: ETB Youthreach Carndonagh
description: Education, training and wellbeing programme for early school leavers aged 15–20.

name: Inishowen Development Partnership (IDP)
description: Supports local communities through training, wellbeing, and family support initiatives.

name: Inishowen Family Action Network (IFAN)
description: Interagency group promoting integrated services for children and families in Inishowen.

name: Carndonagh Community School – Pastoral Care Team
description: Supports students' mental health and wellbeing through mentoring and engagement programmes.

name: Public Health Nurse Network (Carndonagh)
description: Provides maternal and child health services, developmental checks, and support to new parents.

name: Mental Health Ireland
description: Mental Health Ireland is the longest established national mental health charity in Ireland. They are a leading organisation dedicated to promoting mental health and wellbeing and to supporting people with mental health challenges in their recovery journey.

name: Safefood
description: Safefood is the public body dedicated to promoting food safety and healthy eating across the island of Ireland through research, education, scientific cooperation, dietary surveillance and media campaigns

name: Breast Cancer Ireland
description: Breast Cancer Ireland is a registered charity, established to raise significant funding to support pioneering research programmes nationally, as well as to promote education and awareness on the importance of good breast health amongst women of all ages.

name: UCC School of Dentistry
description: The UCC School of Dentistry plays a key role in educating and training dental professionals through its comprehensive undergraduate and postgraduate programmes. It is also dedicated to advancing dental research and providing high-quality patient care through its associated teaching clinics.

name: UCC School of Audiloogy
description: The UCC School of Audiology plays a key role in educating and training future audiologists through a combination of academic coursework, clinical practice, and research. It also contributes to the advancement of hearing healthcare by engaging in community outreach and conducting cutting-edge audiological research

name: RCSI
description: The RCSI School of Physiotherapy educates and trains highly competent, evidence-based physiotherapists through rigorous undergraduate and postgraduate programmes—combining scientific coursework, extensive clinical placements (over one-third of the curriculum), and strong interprofessional learning across disciplines

name: University of Ulster
description: Ulster University School of Health Sciences is one of the largest and oldest providers of allied health professional education in the UK and Ireland, offering undergraduate and postgraduate programmes in six AHP disciplines plus healthcare science, all delivered via interprofessional clinical placements and modern labs

name: Rosemary Collaboratory
description: The Rosemary Collaboratory is a global initiative launched by Special Olympics International that brings together researchers and stakeholders across eight countries (including Ireland) and three U.S. states to assess and advocate for equitable healthcare systems for individuals with intellectual disabilities.

name: Sport Northern Ireland
description: Special Olympics Ulster, operates in close partnership with Sport Northern Ireland (Sport NI), which provides core government funding, strategic support, and recognition for its activities across the region

name: Agirre Lehendakaria Center
description: Agirre Lehendakaria Center is a Social Innovation Laboratory specialized in developing systems thinking capabilities to address complex societal challenges with a developmental evaluation approach. In contrast to the traditional linear and single point evaluation approach, which applies a logical framework to implement activities that are designed to respond to specific, pre-specified goals, social innovations that aim to tackle complex societal challenges such as mental health demands a systemic approach, very different set of data and real time information to enable adaptive management throughout the life cycle of collaborative and multi-agent interventions (portfolio management).

name: Shoutout
description: Promotes inclusion through education by delivering LGBTQ+ Educational Programmes in schools, workplaces and service providers

name: spunout
description: National youth information and support platform

name: Childhood Matters
description: Childhood Matters is a non-profit child & family support service. Our child and family support services are aimed at supporting and promoting positive childhood outcomes, developing & understanding parenting capacity, building overall family resilience, and contributing to healthier and more sustainable communities.

name: Hair Together
description: Empowering young adults through community, creativity, and care. - hairstyling, barbering, and wellness programs

name: Bohemian Football Club
description: Bohemian Football Club is a professional football club based in Phibsborough, Dublin 7. Playing in the League of Ireland, Bohemian F.C. is a 100% fan owned club. The club believes that football can be used a force for good - not only in the local community but in wider society. The club promotes values of leadership, solidarity, integrity, respect and creavity, whilst working across 5 key areas: health & wellbeing, rehabilitation, educations & empowerment, anti-discrimination & inclusion and climate justice & sustainability.

name: Active Connections
description: Active Connections CLG aims to have adventure therapy as an option for all people in need, and at risk in the country. Created in April 2011, it is a not for profit company working with young people with behavioural difficulties, and is leading the charge of introducing adventure therapy based programmes in Ireland. Since inception AC has worked in partnership with statutory and non-statutory agencies, delivering programmes to young people and their families in the regions of Dublin, Cork, Tipperary, Kilkenny, Carlow, Wexford, and Waterford.

name: Football Coop
description: Football Cooperative is a social enterprise established in 2017 dedicated to bringing men together through the medium of recreational football. It is built on the principle of using sport as a catalyst to enhance physical, mental, and social wellbeing. The organisation operates through a network of locally branded sites and is run by volunteer Game Coordinators who are responsible for managing and delivering its activities. The organisation is supported by an advisory board including representatives from UEFA, the FAI, HSE, and Sport Ireland.

name: Irish Homeless Street Leagues
description: Founded in 2004, IHSL is a volunteer driven non profit organisation that uses the power of football to transform the lives of people who have found themselves affected by social exclusion. Through our nationwide leagues we create positive change by motivating, inspiring and developing people who have experienced homelessness, alcohol and substance abuse, or are ex-offenders, long term unemployed and refugees. The annual All Ireland Finals tournament brings together teams from our nationwide leagues to compete and teams are chosen to represent Ireland in the annual international Homeless World Cup.

name: Sanctuary Runners
description:
The Sanctuary Runners enable Irish residents to run alongside, and in solidarity with, migrants, asylum seekers and refugees thereby fostering friendship, positivity and respect while bringing greater awareness to the migration system. 

Established in 2018, SR has groups across Ireland and is  expanding internationally. The model allows communities to get to know each other, fosters community integration and improves physical and mental health.

name: Irish Surfing
description: IRISH SURFING IS THE NATIONAL GOVERNING BODY FOR THE SPORT OF SURFING IN IRELAND.

name: Donegal Sports Partnership
description: DSP provide opportunities for participating in sport and physical activities for individuals and specific target groups especially those most disadvantaged or marginalised

name: Pobal
description: Pobal manages funding and provide support for 38 programmes in the areas of Social Inclusion

name: Donegal County Council
description: Local Authority in Co Donegal

name: Interreg
description: European funding programme to share innovative and sustainable solutions to regional development challenges.

name: National Surf Centre Strandhill
description: National Centre of Excellence for Surfing

name: INCLUSEA
description: INCLUSEA is an EU Consortium aimed to develop and co-create a best practice guide for inclusive and adaptive surfing

name: Rugby Players Ireland Foundation
description: The Rugby Players Ireland Foundation aims to provide financial and practical support for players and their families who find themselves in difficult situations such as career ending injury, illness or disability. As a registered charity the support provided by the Foundation will take many forms including financial assistance, help with medical treatment, rehabilitation equipment, counselling and education. The Foundation provides an emergency support for those who are struggling in various ways while also giving players the opportunity to be part of a ‘players supporting players’ community.

name: Z Zurich Foundation
description: The Z Zurich Foundation is a Swiss-based charitable foundation established by members of the Zurich Insurance Group. It is the main vehicle by which Zurich Insurance Group delivers on its global community investment strategy.

name: Rugby Player Ireland Members
description: Rugby members current and past who act as role models for the Tackle Your Feelings campaign

name: Foróige - The National Youth Development Organisation
description: Foróige is a national youth organisation. Their purpose is to enable young people to involve themselves consciously and actively in their own development, and in the development of society. Active since 1952, today Foroige work with over 50,000 young people aged 10 – 18 each year.

name: ISCOIL
description: iScoil is a non-profit online learning service that offers young people a pathway to learning, accreditation and progression.

name: Sport Ireland
description: Sport Ireland is the authority tasked with the development of sport in Ireland. This includes participation in sport, high performance sport, anti-doping, coaching and the development of the Sport Ireland Campus.

name: DCU anti-bullying centre
description: University designated research centre located in DCU's Institute of Education.

name: No Barriers
description:
No Barriers Foundation is dedicated to breaking down physical and societal barriers through a range of inclusive programmes and services.
At No Barriers, our mission is to break down the barriers that prevent individuals with disabilities from accessing exercise and physical activity.

Founded on the belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to lead a healthy and active life, we are dedicated to creating inclusive programs and environments where physical and mental well-being can thrive

name: Zurich Ireland
description: Zurich Ireland are a donor on the Headstart Fund

name: National Youth Assembly
description: The National Youth Assembly of Ireland is a consultative forum for young people between the ages of 12 – 24 years. It is one of a number of youth participative structures in Ireland to ensure the voice of children and young people are heard by government and decision- makers.

name: Comhairle na nOg
description: Comhairle na nÓg child and youth councils give young people all over Ireland the chance to have their voices heard on the issues affecting their lives, both today and in the future

name: Mental Health Reform
description: Mental Health Reform is Ireland's leading coalition on mental health. Our goal is to promote improved mental health services across the country.

name: Philanthropy Ireland
description: Philanthropy Ireland is a dynamic membership organisation dedicated to advancing philanthropy in Ireland

name: The Lighthouse Charity
description: Lighthouse Charity offers Free 24/7 Emotional, Physical & Financial Wellbeing Support for Construction Workers & their Families

name: State Street
description: State Street offers a comprehensive range of services, including investment servicing, investment management, research, and trading, catering primarily to institutional investors worldwide. It's a key supporter of Rethink Ireland initiatives.

name: Nature Worx
description: CLG is an Irish non-profit organization dedicated to promoting positive mental health and well-being through nature-based activities. Established in June 2021, the organization collaborates with research groups and universities to develop evidence-based programs for individuals facing health challenges, mental health difficulties, and psychosocial needs. Awardee supported by Rethink Ireland in various impact areas.

name: Bounce Back Recycling
description: Bounce Back Recycling is a social enterprise in Ireland that specializes in recycling mattresses and furniture, while creating employment opportunities for the Traveller community.

name: UNESCO Child and Family Research Centre at the University of Galway
description:
Rethink Ireland worked with the UNESCO Child
and Family Research Centre at the
University of Galway over a six-year
period to execute academic
evaluations of Rethink Ireland
funds supporting projects tackling
education inequality and youth
mental health. The evaluations
were drafted by the end of 2024
and the findings of the report were
published in early 2025

name: Rainbow Club
description: The Rainbow Club is a lifeline to families, children, young teens and adults living with Autism. We support their needs in coping with the world and challenges they face on a daily basis.

name: Eco Unesco
description: promotes the personal development of young people by developing and channelling the passion that young people feel about the environment into positive and creative actions to protect the natural world

name: Department of Children, Disability and Equality
description: Working to enhance the lives of children, young people, adults, families, and communities by promoting diversity and equality of opportunity

name: Department of Education and Youth
description: Develops and implements education and youth policies, sets standards, and provides essential services for the Irish education system from early learning to higher education and youth services

name: YouthMedInfo
description: Provider of reliable information on the most commonly used mental health medicines for young people.

name: Young social innovators
description: hosts an annual "social awareness and active citizenship and education programme" for 15-18 year old students from Ireland.

name: Higher education authority
description: statutory body responsible for the strategic development and governance of the higher education and research system

name: Gaisce
description: Ireland's national youth award, a self-directed, non-competitive personal development program for young people aged 14–25

name: bodywhys
description: National charity for prevention and treatment of eating disorders

name: Atlantic Futures
description: A collaborative, cross-border, four-year research project funded by the North-South Research Programme, involving universities in the North West of Ireland and Northern Ireland to foster innovation and address regional inequalities in the North West Atlantic Innovation Corridor region.

name: Pieta
description: Provides professional and compassionate support for individuals in suicidal distress or engaging in self-harm.

name: Barnardos
description: Provides support services to vulnerable children and families across Ireland

name: Samaritans
description: Offers emotional support 24 hours a day, 365 days a year, to anyone who is in distress, lonely, struggling to cope

name: HUGG
description: Support for people bereaved by suicide

name: Shona project
description: Educates, empowers and inspires today's girls in Ireland to become tomorrow's strong, confident, and curious young women

name: Aware
description: Aware are the national organisation providing free support, education and information services to people impacted by anxiety, depression, bipolar disorder and related mood conditions. Please select an option below to find out more about the organisation.

name: Common Knowledge Centre for Sustainable Living CLG
description: Common Knowledge is a non-profit social enterprise located at the gateway to the Burren, on Ireland’s beautiful Wild Atlantic Way. We share skills in building, making, mending and growing, because we believe that between us all, we have the knowledge needed to create a truly sustainable future.

name: Anew Support Services CLG
description: Anew is the only charity in Ireland that works specifically with pregnant women and new mothers experiencing homelessness

name: Childhood Development Initiative
description: Not-for-profit based in Fettercairn, Tallaght, Dublin 24, supporting services across the country.Dedicated to improving the lives of children, families, and communities in Tallaght and Ireland, every child in Ireland deserves to be thriving, healthy, happy and free from poverty, and CDI is dedicated to making this happen.

name: Spiritan Asylum Services Initiative CLG (T/A Spirasi)
description: Ireland’s national centre for the rehabilitation of victims of torture.

name: Women's Collective Ireland - Tallaght
description: WCI Tallaght supports grassroots women through collective and practical actions to achieve their full human rights and true equality. We work directly with women on the ground to build women’s confidence and self-esteem through Feminist Community Education and Community development, empowering women to have a voice on women’s issues.

name: Offaly Traveller Movement
description: Offaly Traveller Movement (OTM) is a Traveller led community development organisation that aims to achieve full equality for the Traveller community in County Offaly. We offer various programmes and services such as health promotion, mental health, accommodation, youth, and education. Our organisation supports Travellers to reach their full potential and develop initiatives to tackle poverty, social exclusion, disadvantage, and discrimination.

name: Street Feast (t/a Neighbourhood Network)
description: Neighbourhood Network came from the team behind Street Feast, a national neighbourhood street party event to celebrate community. We are community development charity organisation. We’re all about building and strengthening neighbourhoods and kick-starting ideas for positive community participation.

name: National Youth Council of Ireland
description: NYCI is the representative body for voluntary youth organisations in Ireland. We use our collective expertise to act on issues that impact on young people.

name: Mason Hayes & Curran LLP
description: Mason Hayes & Curran LLP is a leading Irish law firm based in Dublin with offices in San Francisco, New York and London. Mason Hayes & Curran LLP are a financial partner with Rethink Ireland currently supporting the Connected Communities Fund.

name: The Department of Rural and Community Development and the Gaeltacht
description: The Department of Rural and Community Development promote rural and community development and to support vibrant, inclusive and sustainable communities throughout Ireland including in the Gaeltacht regions.

name: Accessible Adventures Ireland
description:
To make adventurous activities in Ireland accessible to everyone by overcoming physical barriers and challenging thinking that excludes people with a disability through the provision of adaptive activities and high quality training. 

https://www.accessibleadventures.ie/

name: Active Disabilities Ireland
description:
Active Disability Ireland are a national pan-disability organisation committed to creating an Ireland where people with disabilities have more opportunity to participate in physical activity of their choice.

https://activedisability.ie/

name: Vision Sports Ireland
description:
Promoting Sport and Physical Activity for people who are Blind/Vision Impaired of all ages and abilities.

https://visionsports.ie/

name: Irish Foster Care Association
description:
The Irish Foster Care Association is the national organisation that supports foster families and the wider fostering community.  Established in 1981 by a group of foster carers and social workers our primary objective as an organisation is to promote excellence in foster care.

https://ifca.ie/

name: Open Specturrm
description:
OPEN SPECTRUM is a parent-run activities club for children and young adults to the age of 30 years who are on the autistic spectrum in South Dublin. Our club was founded in 2013 by Helen Norris and Victoria White, two parents of teens on the spectrum who met at an autism-friendly event. They felt their children should have the same opportunities to take part in after-school activities that their “normal” children had but could not find a club to serve their needs so they decided to set one up.

https://openspectrum.ie/about-us/
